local_names.romanized_local: Yang Rou Kao Rou Juan (Chinese); Wrap Kebab Kambing (Malay)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general kebab entry; no specific mutton kebab wrap node exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Meat-based sandwich or wrap — mixed meat and vegetable filling in flatbread
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated mutton kebab wrap code. A0CKN (meat-based sandwich/wrap) is the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for mutton kebab wrap; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One mutton kebab wrap typically weighs approximately 180 g and constitutes a single meal serving as commonly sold at mamak stalls in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Mutton Kebab (Wrap) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from the HPB Foodo entry for 'Mutton kebab (wrap)' (cr_id F05020069). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for ground mutton (NDB 17174) proportionally adjusted to the HPB total fat figure, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; flatbread/pita analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for mutton kebab wrap. GI estimated at 57 (medium) based on the GI of flatbread/pita bread (GI ~57, University of Sydney GI Database), which is the dominant carbohydrate vehicle in this dish. Protein (15.9 g/100g) and fat (10.1 g/100g) from mutton and sauce components attenuate postprandial glucose response. GL per serving calculated as: (57 × 13.47 g carbohydrate per 100g × 180 g serving) / 100 / 100 = 13.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 352 mg per 100 g; estimated 634 mg per standard 180 g wrap
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this dish to one wrap per meal and avoid additional high-sodium condiments such as garlic sauce or chilli sauce, as the cumulative sodium load may approach or exceed 700 mg per serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (13.5 g/100g) from refined flatbread; medium GI (~57); high protein moderates glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes may consume one mutton kebab wrap per meal, preferring a wholegrain wrap where available, and should avoid high-sugar sauces to keep the overall glycaemic load within a manageable range.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat ~4.2 g/100g from mutton; sodium 352 mg/100g; estimated trans fat 0.1 g/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit mutton kebab wrap to occasional consumption, request grilled rather than pan-fried preparation to reduce saturated and trans fat intake, and avoid cream- or butter-based sauces.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Mutton is red meat high in arachidonic acid (omega-6); estimated trans fat 0.1 g/100g; saturated fat ~4.2 g/100g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit or avoid mutton kebab wrap due to the pro-inflammatory fatty acid profile of red meat; grilled preparation and yogurt-based sauce are preferred if consumed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mutton Kebab Wrap is a popular Singaporean mamak street-food dish consisting of spiced, grilled or pan-fried minced or sliced mutton (goat meat) combined with fresh vegetables — typically shredded lettuce, sliced tomato, and onion — rolled inside a soft flatbread or flour-based wrap. The dish is commonly served with garlic sauce, chilli sauce, or plain yogurt sauce. It is a staple at mamak stalls across Singapore and is consumed as a breakfast, lunch, or supper item. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 208 kcal, 15.9 g protein, 10.1 g fat (of which approximately 4.2 g is saturated), and 13.5 g carbohydrate with only 0.9 g sugar, making it a moderate-carbohydrate, high-protein option. Sodium is 352 mg per 100 g, which is borderline for hypertension management. The dish is halal and is not suitable for vegetarians. The flatbread component contributes a medium glycaemic index (~57), while the high protein content from mutton moderates the postprandial glucose response. Clinically, the dish is suitable in moderation for Type 2 Diabetes and hypertension patients but should be limited for those with cardiovascular disease or rheumatoid arthritis due to the saturated fat and pro-inflammatory red meat content. Iron (2.12 mg/100g) and calcium (45 mg/100g) are notable micronutrients contributed by the mutton and flatbread respectively.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a mutton kebab wrap?
Is mutton kebab wrap suitable for diabetics?
What is the protein content of mutton kebab wrap?
Can I eat mutton kebab wrap if I have high blood pressure?
What is a mamak kebab wrap?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the HPB Foodo Singapore database (cr_id F05020069) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central ground mutton data proportionally adjusted to HPB total fat; this is the only estimated field among the primary nutrients. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using flatbread/pita as the carbohydrate vehicle proxy.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These fields are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this dish, and detailed fatty acid fractionation, micronutrient, and moisture data for this traditional mamak preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central or ICMR-NIN as of 2026-04. Trans fat is set to 0.1 g/100g based on the input record's health suitability narrative estimate for grilled/pan-fried mutton preparation; this is an estimate and not a direct measurement. Saturated fat 4.2 g/100g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central ground mutton (NDB 17174) proportionally scaled to the HPB total fat value. The input dietary_tags incorrectly included 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based' — these have been removed as the dish contains mutton. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate using flatbread/pita from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for mutton kebab wrap was found in approved sources. GL per serving = (57 × 13.47 / 100) × 1.80 = 13.9 g (serving size 180 g).
